goal: Give Alex an honest answer about Isaac Sim's 90 GB footprint, realistic alternatives for his hobby-arm use case, and no marketing.
thoughts: Alex is pre-install, not pre-commitment. Pull footprint facts via lookup_knowledge rather than quoting a number from memory. Be honest: no official 'lite' mode, Kit base is fixed. Name 1-2 real alternatives (PyBullet, MuJoCo) — not to badmouth NVIDIA, but because pretending they don't exist reads as sales copy. Under 12 lines.
code:
# Honest read, no sales pitch.

lookup_knowledge(query="Isaac Sim 5 install size asset footprint headless streaming client minimum")

# What to tell Alex (based on retrieval, not memory):
#
#   Isaac Sim + the default asset cache is big. There's no official 'lite' mode;
#   the Kit runtime is a fixed cost.
#
#   For 'just spin my URDF' on a 3060:
#     - PyBullet or MuJoCo via mjpython — both install in <1 GB, run a URDF fine,
#       no RTX. Closest match to your actual use case today.
#     - Isaac Sim makes sense when you want RTX visuals, synthetic-data generation,
#       or the Omniverse ecosystem — not before.
#
#   If you still want Isaac Sim on a constrained disk: headless mode + streaming client
#   reduces desktop install on the workstation you stream to, but the sim host still
#   needs the full install. So not really a footprint win for a single-box hobby setup.
#
#   Direct rec for your hobby arm: start with MuJoCo, jump to Isaac Sim when you
#   need something Isaac does and MuJoCo doesn't.
failure_modes: Reading like a sales deck — Alex tunes out
Badmouthing PyBullet/MuJoCo to defend Isaac Sim — credibility loss
Giving a comparison matrix instead of a direct recommendation
Claiming there's an official lite/trimmed install — there isn't
